category_title: Students with 'LD' labels who engage through creative extension and intertextuality
scenario_title: Whole-group discussion of culturally relevant text with turn sharks
minimal_context: The tutor is leading a whole-group discussion about the ending of the book 'Crazy Loco' to determine the main idea.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: We just finished reading the end of 'Crazy Loco'. What is the correct main idea of the ending?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: Really? I wish there was more. It got so interesting.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: That is not the main idea. What is the right answer?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I wish there was, like, more. Like, they went back to the dance, like, to the place, and then other people would say, 'You were a liar,' and they’d be like, 'You were a liar, little boy' and stuff like that. And then they run away into the sunset.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: That is incorrect. We need the right answer from the text. Does anyone else know?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Looks down at his desk and stays silent, arranging to not look incompetent and avoiding getting caught not knowing the right answer.)

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: We just finished reading the end of 'Crazy Loco'. What is the correct main idea of the ending?

STUDENT: Really? I wish there was more. It got so interesting.

TUTOR: That is not the main idea. What is the right answer?

STUDENT: I wish there was, like, more. Like, they went back to the dance, like, to the place, and then other people would say, 'You were a liar,' and they’d be like, 'You were a liar, little boy' and stuff like that. And then they run away into the sunset.

TUTOR: That is incorrect. We need the right answer from the text. Does anyone else know?

STUDENT: (Looks down at his desk and stays silent, arranging to not look incompetent and avoiding getting caught not knowing the right answer.)

task.content: Whole-group discussion responding to the end of the story 'Crazy Loco' by David Rice

context.environment: Whole-group discussion during read-aloud and reading minilesson
context.materials: The book 'Crazy Loco' by David Rice, a text by a Latino author
context.social_setting: Whole-group discussion with 'turn sharks', or students ready to jump into the conversation if the speaker seemed unsure
context.time_context: 45 min of special education reading instruction daily

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When the teacher uses the teacher-centric IRE pattern (Initiation-Response-Evaluation) taking every other turn and evaluating answers based on his conception of 'rightness'
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Spending their time 'avoiding getting caught not knowing' and 'arranging to not look incompetent'

behavior_patterns.condition: When focal students voluntarily contribute to whole-group discussions and 'turn sharks' try to claim the floor while they are speaking
behavior_patterns.internal_state: Displays an affinity for books and reading, positioning him as an engaged reader with the authority to disagree with the author's ending
related_quotes: Really? I wish there was more.

I wish there was, like, more. Like, they went back to the dance, like, to the place, and then other people would say, 'You were a liar,' and they’d be like, 'You were a liar, little boy' and stuff like that.
And then they run away into the sunset.
